name: Flipkart Sorted Feed Merger
scenario: Flipkart receives K sorted product price feeds from K suppliers. Merge all into one sorted list using a min-heap.

core.description: mergeKSorted(lists): PriorityQueue min-heap O(N log K). Test: 3 lists merged correctly.
sortList(head): merge sort O(n log n) O(log n). Test: [4,2,1,3]→[1,2,3,4].
README: explains why merge sort not quicksort for LL.

bonus.description: sortListBottomUp: O(n log n) O(1) space (iterative merge sort, no recursion stack).
insertionSortList: O(n²) but stable and useful for nearly-sorted LL.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. mergeKSorted: PriorityQueue<ListNode> pq with comparator (a,b)->a.val-b.val. Push all non-null heads. Poll min, push min.next if not null. Collect into dummy chain. sortList: find middle (fast=head.next), slow.next=null to split. Recurse on both halves. mergeTwoSorted on results. fast=head.next (not head): for [1,2], fast.next=null immediately → slow=1, mid=2 (correct split). If fast=head: for [1,2], fast.next.next=null → slow=2, mid=null (wrong!). reorderList: (1) find middle fast=head, (2) reverse slow.next, (3) interleave: while right!=null: next1=left.next, next2=right.next, left.next=right, right.next=next1, left=next1, right=next2.
